title: NOC 21233 – Web Designers | GTR Immigration
description: Web Designers are in strong demand across Canada, especially in digital hubs such as Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal. Classified under NOC 21233 (TEER 1), this occupation supports immigration through skilled-worker programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), employer-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2123 – Computer, software, and web designers and developers

duties: Establish design standards and visual guidelines
Create visual imagery consistent with organizational identity
Integrate interactive features and site functionality
Develop web content using graphics, animation, and databases
Test and improve website usability and layout
Coordinate multidisciplinary design teams when required
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, graphic design, or web development
Or a college diploma in graphic arts, computer science, or web design
job_outlook: This keeps Web Designers well-positioned for immigration, especially candidates with:
additional_information: Canada’s expanding digital economy, fintech sector, marketing agencies, and media companies continue to drive hiring for:
UI/UX designersFront-end specialistsDigital-branding designersWebsite-optimization professionalsInteractive-media creators
Annual earnings typically range between $34,632–$96,200 CAD, depending on experience level, province, and technical specialization.
